2606.20812.title: B[FM]$^2$: Brain Foundation Model via Flow Matching with SplitUNet
2606.20812.paper_summary: EEG foundation models can learn generalizable representations from large-scale EEG corpora to enable single-backbone transfer across diverse clinical and brain-computer interface tasks. Existing models typically discretize the continuous multi-channel EEG waveform into patches or codebook tokens and train a transformer with masked self-supervision. Recognizing that this discretization fragments continuous brain rhythms and obscures fine-grained temporal dynamics, we present B[FM]$^2$(Brain Foundation Model via Flow Matching), whose inductive bias aligns with the data by pretraining directly on the raw signal using continuous-time flow matching without patches, tokenization, or masking. However, multi-channel EEG signals pose an architectural challenge for flow matching: time is densely sampled and highly autocorrelated (thousands of timepoints), while the electrode axis is short (tens of channels) at distinct scalp positions. To address this time-electrode asymmetry, we introduce SplitUNet, a velocity network that factorizes each block into separate 1D temporal and 1D electrode convolutions and downsamples only along time, preserving electrode topology throughout the hierarchy. B[FM]$^2$ sets a new state of the art on 7 of 9 standard downstream EEG classification tasks, using a pretraining budget of only 36,895 segments ($\approx$ 307h), 1-2 orders of magnitude ($\approx$ 30x) less than required by existing EEG foundation models. Further, it generates synthetic EEGs that two board-certified neurologists cannot distinguish from brain data (Cohen's $κ=$ -0.096). https://jd730.github.io/projects/BFM2
2606.20812.paper_summary_zh: EEG 基礎模型可以從大規模 EEG 資料庫中學習可泛化的表示，以實現跨多樣臨床和腦-電腦介面任務的單一骨幹轉移。現有模型通常將連續的多通道 EEG 波形離散化為補丁或代碼本標記，並使用遮蔽自我監督訓練Transformer。認識到這種離散化會破壞連續的腦節律並模糊細微的時間動態，我們提出了 B[FM]$^2$（通過流匹配的腦基礎模型），其歸納偏差通過直接在原始信號上進行預訓練來與數據對齊，使用連續時間流匹配而不需要補丁、標記化或遮蔽。然而，多通道 EEG 信號對流匹配提出了架構挑戰：時間密集取樣且高度自相關（數千個時間點），而電極軸在不同的頭皮位置上則較短（數十個通道）。為了解決這種時間-電極不對稱，我們引入了 SplitUNet，一種速度網絡，將每個區塊分解為獨立的 1D 時間和 1D 電極卷積，並僅沿時間進行下採樣，保留整個層級中的電極拓撲。B[FM]$^2$ 在 9 個標準下游 EEG 分類任務中的 7 個任務上創造了新的技術標杆，使用的預訓練預算僅為 36,895 段（約 307 小時），比現有的 EEG 基礎模型所需的少 1-2 個數量級（約 30 倍）。此外，它生成的合成 EEG 使兩位董事認證的神經科醫生無法與腦數據區分開來（Cohen's $κ=$ -0.096）。 https://jd730.github.io/projects/BFM2
2606.20812.author: Jaedong Hwang et.al.
2606.20812.authors: Jaedong Hwang, Kathleen Zhang, Wei Dai, Konstantinos Kontras, Maarten Vanmarcke, Maarten De Vos, Ila Fiete, Paul Pu Liang
